question:
Will Bilibili Gaming win LoL Worlds 2025?

Description: This market will resolve according to the esports team winning 1st place in the League of Legends 2025 Season World Championship (Worlds 2025).

If the winner of the World Cup 2025 is not determined by December 31, 2025 ET, this market will resolve to "Other".

The resolution source for this market will be official information from Liquipedia: https://liquipedia.net/leagueoflegends/Main_Page.

evidences.title: Team Bilibili Gaming LoL – Bilibili Gaming League of Legends Esports Team Stats, Roster, Matches & Results
evidences.content: The year 2024 was historic for Bilibili Gaming. The team strengthened its lineup by signing Knight for mid lane, replacing Yagao. In Spring 2024, they won the LPL Spring title for the first time, defeating Top Esports 3:1 in the final. That victory earned them a spot at MSI 2024, where they once again reached the final and finished second after losing to Gen.G. In Summer 2024, they confirmed their top-team status by winning LPL Summer even more decisively, sweeping Weibo Gaming 3:0 in the final. At Worlds 2024, the team reached the grand final, where they narrowly lost to T1 in a dramatic 2:3 series. It was the best international result in Bilibili Gaming’s history. ## Bilibili Gaming Initial Roster [...] This Bilibili Gaming roster has achieved major success in recent seasons. Together, they won both the Spring and Summer splits of the LPL in 2024. In Spring, they defeated Top Esports in the final with a score of 3:1, and in Summer they convincingly beat Weibo Gaming 3:0. Beyond domestic victories, the roster reached the final of the 2024 Mid-Season Invitational, finishing second after a 1:3 loss to Gen.G. An even more significant accomplishment was reaching the 2024 World Cham...

evidences.title: Bilibili Gaming secures Worlds Spot as LPL 2024 season surpasses 2023 viewership | Esports Charts
evidences.content: The LPL Summer 2024 witnessed all 17 teams from theSpring split battle it out for a prize pool of $580,000. Unlike previous iterations, it featured a Group Stage, Rumble Stage, and the use of the 'Fearless Draft' feature in the Group Stage, where teams were split into four pots based on results from the previous season. Bilibili Gaming won its second straight LPL trophy Bilibili Gaming won its second straight LPL trophy (Image via LPL on X) After almost three months of action,Bilibili Gaming beatWeibo Gaming 3:0 in the Grand Final to clinch its second straight split. The winner, who has recently been on a roll, earned a spot in the Swiss Stage of the 2024 World Championship aside from pocketing $275,000. [...] Bilibili Gaming also became the fourth team to win both LPL splits in a year with this victory in Nanjing. Moreover, knight created history as the first player to win four straight titles in this series, with his two titles last year coming with JD Gaming, who was on a record-equalling run of claiming three straight splits. This League of Legends Pro League competition peaked at 208K concurrent viewers during the summit clash, doing 64% better than any other game. These tw...

evidences.title: LoL Worlds 2025: Teams and seeding confirmed
evidences.content: Fnatic qualified for the League of Legends Championship last week for the ninth consecutive year, ending the season of Karmine Corp with a 3-1 victory. ## Last 10 League of Legends world champions ## Format and dates The League of Legends World Championship 2025 starts with the Play-In between Invictus Gaming and T1 on October 14th. Then 16 teams will take part in a five-round Swiss Stage format between October 15th and October 25th. The top eight teams will then play in a single elimination bracket from October 28th until the Grand Final on November 9th. author avatar author avatar Kieran Lynch, Senior Editor [...] ## Notable teams in the LOL Worlds 2025 Defending champions T1 will have to go through a Play-In to reach the Swiss Stage of the League of Legends World Championship. They will face Invictus Gaming on Tuesday, October 14th, with the winner joining 15 other teams in the Swiss Stage. Bilibili Gaming will hope to go one better than 2024, after losing 3-2 to T1 in last year’s final. Their roster includes Jungler Zhao ‘Shad0w’ Zhiqiang, who became the first European to win the LPL in China. Shad0w is an Italian pro having been born in Milan to a family of Chinese heritage...

evidences.title: Worlds 2025 Primer - LoL Esports
evidences.content: PSG Talon (PSG) WORLDS FORMAT Video 1 SWISS STAGE DRAWS Right after the Play-In Stage wraps, the action jumps straight into the Swiss Draws, with matchups revealed live and the first sparks of Worlds drama igniting. Round 1 (October 14) The first round is all based on seeding. This year, we’ve changed up our draw tiers and added in a new twist - the Play-In result doesn’t just decide who qualifies to Swiss, it also bumps that region’s #3 seed into Tier 2. Teams are split into three tiers: Tier 1 (5 Teams): #1 seed from each region Tier 2 (6 Teams): #2 seed from each region + the #3 seed from the Play-In winner’s region Tier 3 (5 Teams): Remaining #3 seeds + the Play-In winner Matchups will be: Tier 1 vs Tier 3 Tier 2 vs Tier 2 [...] Play-In Stage (Bo5) - Beijing(All matches begin at 4 p.m. CST / 10 a.m. CET / 1 a.m. PT unless otherwise noted) Tuesday, October 14 Swiss Stage (Bo1, Bo3)- Beijing (All matches begin at 4 p.m. CST / 10 a.m. CET / 1 a.m. PT unless otherwise noted) Wednesday, October 15 - 1 p.m. CST / 7 a.m. CET / 10 p.m. PT Oct. 14 Thursday, October 16 - 1 p.m. CST / 7 a.m. CET / 10 p.m. PT Oct. 15 Friday, October 17 Saturday, October 18 Sunday, October 19 Wednesday,...

evidences.title: Worlds 2025 Heads to China! - LoL Esports
evidences.content: Worlds 2025 begins with 17 teams vying for the trophy. The Play-In Stage will kick off October 14 in Beijing at the state-of-the-art Beijing Smart Esports Center, a venue built specifically for premier esports events. This will be the first time the Beijing Smart Esports Center hosts a World Championship, and it promises to deliver an electric atmosphere. New this year, the Play-In Stage will host a one-day best of five between the fourth seeded team of the two best performing regions from MSI to punch their ticket to the Swiss Stage. From October 15-25, the Swiss Stage will bring together 16 teams from across the globe in a dynamic format, where every win moves you closer to knockout contention—and every loss brings you closer to elimination. [...] Riot Games League of Legends © 2026 Riot Games. All Rights Reserved. Privacy NoticeTerms of ServiceAccessibility The 2025 League of Legends World Championship is heading back to China and will take place from October 14 to November 9. Fans around the world can look forward to world-class competition, unforgettable moments, and the crowning of the next World Champion. ### Play-In and Swiss Stages – Beijing Smart Esports Center, Beijing

evidences.content: Riot Games League of Legends © 2026 Riot Games. Todos os direitos reservados. Aviso de PrivacidadeTermos de ServiçoAcessibilidade O início de uma nova temporada incrível do LoL Esports chegou, trazendo novidades empolgantes, novos formatos e um ambicioso roteiro de eventos globais que promete momentos inesquecíveis para fãs do mundo inteiro. A temporada de 2025 começa oficialmente nas seguintes datas regionais: LPL: 12 de janeiro LCK: 15 de janeiro LCP: 17 de janeiro LEC: 18 de janeiro LTA: 25 de janeiro Etapa 1 [...] Campeonato Mundial de 2025 O Mundial acontecerá em algumas das cidades mais icônicas da China. A Fase de Entrada e a Fase Suíça darão início ao campeonato em Pequim, enquanto as Quartas de Final e as Semifinais acontecerão em Xangai, culminando com a Grande Final em Chengdu. Vamos compartilhar mais detalhes sobre locais do evento em breve, mas uma novidade desse ano que já podemos anunciar é a eliminação da semana de intervalo entre as Quartas de Final e as Semifinais. Muitos fãs compartilharam que sentem que o Mundial perde o embalo durante esse período, então fizemos mudanças e estamos empolgados para apresentar uma fase atrás da outra, sem deixar que a ação pare...

evidences.title: Worlds Standings - LoL Esports
evidences.content: ## Worlds Standings Oct – Nov ##### Worlds overview The top teams from the season come together for the final tournament of the year. The winner is crowned the best team in the world. 1st Place ###### 1st Place Winner of the 2025 League of Legends Esports season ##### Event Phases The top teams from the season come together for the final tournament of the year. The winner is crowned the best team in the world. ###### Play-In The 4th seed of the regions that obtained an additional slot from MSI will compete in the Play-in stage. The Play-In stage is 1 single elimination best of 5 that qualifies 1 teams into Swiss Stage. ###### Swiss 16 teams comes together to play in a 5 round Swiss format. The 8 teams with 3 wins at the end of round 5 will advance to the Knockout stage. [...] ###### Knockout 8 teams compete in a single elimination bracket to crown the winner of the 2025 season. ##### Qualifying Teams The following teams have qualified CBLOLCBLOL VKS VKS LCSLCS FLY FLY LECLEC G2 G2 MKOI MKOI FNC FNC LCPLCP CFO CFO PSG PSG TSW TSW LCKLCK KT KT HLE HLE GEN GEN T1 T1 LPLLPL LTA NorthLTA North Riot Games League of Legends © 2026 Riot Games. evidences.title: Dot Esports' LoL Worlds 2023 power rankings
evidences.content: ### 5) Bilibili Gaming Although Bilibili Gaming definitely could have finished higher than third in the LPL playoffs, they did have the best record in the league this summer, losing just one regular split match to JDG—the same team that prevented them from lifting the trophy at this year’s MSI. Bilibili are quiet, consistent winners, but they struggle to get past the teams that have had their number. Decisive playoff losses to JDG and LNG have created a solid wall between Bilibili and our top four, but we’re not willing to count them out of the title conversation. ### 4) LNG Esports [...] ### 6) KT Rolster KT are back at Worlds for the first time in five years. Back then, the team was a hard-lined favorite to win the whole thing but ultimately came up short in the quarterfinal round. While the expectations might not be as high this time around, we expect KT—a legacy org that’s finally back in the picture—to be a contender in the later stages of the tournament. Veterans like Bdd and Lehends have both made the top four at Worlds before, while the topside duo of Kiin and Cuzz have enough power between them to warrant faith in an organization that’s had little to believe in for the...
